zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II於1460年5月15日出生在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾莫頓時，他的父親William Gordon First of Lochinvar40歲，母親Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton31歲。他和Lady Janet Crichton至少有1個兒子。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II于1460年5月15日出生在英国苏格兰度福利爾莫頓时，他的父亲William Gordon First of Lochinvar40岁，母亲Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton31岁。他和Lady Janet Crichton至少有1个儿子。他去世于1513年9月9日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gordon,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Gordon in Berwickshire, named with Welsh gor ‘spacious’ + din ‘fort’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Gourdon in Saône-et-Loire, so called from the Gallo-Roman personal name Gordus + the locative suffix -o, -ōnis.
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, said to be a nickname from a diminutive of Old French gourd ‘heavy, dull, sluggish’ (compare 8 below).

variants.name: Gordin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eyal, Moisey, Yakov, Zalman, Erez, Faina, Haya, Mendel.  Russian Leonid, Aleksandr, Boris, Feiga, Genady.
Jewish (from Russia): variant of   Gordon   5.
French: variant of   Gourdin  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II was born on 15 May 1460, in Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, William Gordon First of Lochinvar, was 40 and his mother, Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton, was 31. He had at least 1 son with Lady Janet Crichton. He died on 9 September 1513, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II est né le 15 mai 1460, à Morton, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Gordon First of Lochinvar, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady Janet Crichton. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1513, à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II am 15. Mai 1460 in Morton, Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Gordon First of Lochinvar 40 und seine Mutter Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton 31. Er hatte mit Lady Janet Crichton mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II è nato il il 15 maggio 1460 nel Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Gordon First of Lochinvar, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady Janet Crichton. Morì il 9 settembre 1513, nel Branxton, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar IIが1460年5月15日にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアモートンで生まれた時，父William Gordon First of Lochinvarは40歳，母Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Mortonは31歳であった。彼はLady Janet Crichtonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1513年9月9日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドブランクストンで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II(이)가 1460년 5월 15일 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 모르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Gordon First of Lochinvar(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Lady Janet Crichton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II nasceu em 15 de maio de 1460, em Morton, Dumfriesshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Gordon First of Lochinvar, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady Janet Crichton. Ele faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1513, em Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II родился 15 мая 1460 г. в местности мортон, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Gordon First of Lochinvar было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady Janet Crichton. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.
